key_insight: The paper shows that enforcing geometry-inspired symmetry in convolutional autoencoders makes latent spaces more stable and better suited for reduced-order modeling of PDE solution manifolds.
engineering_relevance: This improves ROM predictability and robustness for fast surrogate simulation of parametric PDEs, especially when nonlinear dynamics are hard to compress with linear methods like POD.
method: Design symmetric convolutional autoencoders that preserve manifold-parameterization properties.
Extend representation consistency from fully connected autoencoders to convolutional layers.
Integrate the symmetric CAE into a ROM pipeline and compare against classical CAEs.
evidence_signal: On three 1D test cases—linear advection, viscous Burgers, and Kuramoto–Sivashinsky—the symmetric CAE ROMs reported lower reconstruction errors, more accurate latent trajectories, and improved robustness.
watch_item: The gains are demonstrated on 1D academic benchmarks, so performance and training stability may differ on higher-dimensional or more complex PDE settings.
paper_title: Convolutional Symmetric AutoEncoders: enhancing latent stability via differential geometry
